title: "Cross-sectional analyses"
author: "Beatrice Cornu Hewitt"
date: "`r Sys.time()`"
output: html_document
---

# Load packages 
```{r}
# Check R version 
R.version.string # R version 4.4.2 (2024-10-31 ucrt)

# Package installer/loader
install_and_load_packages <- function() {
  # Core CRAN packages
  cran_pkgs <- c(
    "phyloseq","vegan","tidyr","dplyr","ggplot2","tidyverse","microbiomer",
    "paletteer","microbiome","devtools","forcats","haven","openxlsx","ggsignif",
    "patchwork","decontam","RColorBrewer","lubridate","stringr","janitor",
    "gtsummary","gt","cowplot","scales","car","lmtest","rcartocolor","broom",
    "writexl","tibble","glue","corrplot","reshape2","Hmisc","fpc","dendextend",
    "ggdendro","purrr","ggh4x","ggpubr","ape","microViz","ggside","Maaslin2"
  )
  # Bioconductor packages
  bioc_pkgs <- c("ANCOMBC","mia")
  # Ensure BiocManager is available
  if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
    install.packages("BiocManager", dependencies = TRUE)
  # Helper to install and load CRAN packages
  for (pkg in cran_pkgs) {
    if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) {
      message("Installing CRAN package: ", pkg)
      install.packages(pkg, dependencies = TRUE)
    }
    suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(pkg, character.only = TRUE))
  }
  # Helper to install and load Bioconductor packages
  for (pkg in bioc_pkgs) {
    if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) {
      message("Installing Bioconductor package: ", pkg)
      BiocManager::install(pkg, ask = FALSE, update = FALSE)
    }
    suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(pkg, character.only = TRUE))
  }
  message("All required packages successfully installed and loaded.")
}

# Run loader 
install_and_load_packages()

# Load my custom functions from scripts folder
source("0_functions.R")
```
# Load data - VGO3 OP and NP 
```{r}
# Load phyloseq
ps <- readRDS("../Input/Phyloseq/VGO3_NPOP/1_ps_raw.Rds")
VGO3_NPOP <- ps

# only keep control control participants - exclude GF and GP samples - keep blanks etc.
VGO3_NPOP <- subset_samples(VGO3_NPOP, !(population %in% c("GF", "GP")))
table(VGO3_NPOP@sam_data$niche) 
 #  NP   OP   BD   BP   FB ZMCB ZMCD  UMD   BB 
 # 978  989  152   17  101   87   29   26    3
table(VGO3_NPOP@sam_data$population) # CP 1941

# Exclude duplicated participants
# Extract metadata
meta <- data.frame(sample_data(VGO3_NPOP))
meta$SampleID <- sample_names(VGO3_NPOP)

# Mark duplicates - only within CP population AND niche is OP or NP
meta <- meta %>%
  mutate(dup = population == "CP" & niche %in% c("OP", "NP") &
           (duplicated(paste(VGO3_ID, niche)) | duplicated(paste(VGO3_ID, niche), fromLast = TRUE)))

# Keep non-duplicates or samples outside the CP+OP/NP filter
keep_samples <- meta$SampleID[!meta$dup]

# Prune phyloseq object
VGO3_NPOP_dedup <- prune_samples(keep_samples, VGO3_NPOP)

# Confirm counts
cat("Samples before:", nsamples(VGO3_NPOP), "\n") # 2397 
cat("Samples after deduplication:", nsamples(VGO3_NPOP_dedup), "\n") # 2364 


# Realised that some niche values are assigned as NP or OP but they are actually the blanks for these niches 
# Filter for rows where niche is NP or OP but VGO3_ID is NOT a 5-digit number
meta_check <- data.frame(VGO3_NPOP_dedup@sam_data)

bad_niche_assignments <- meta_check %>%
  filter(niche %in% c("NP", "OP") & !grepl("^\\d{5}$", VGO3_ID))

# Print how many problematic rows there are
cat("Number of samples with niche NP or OP but invalid VGO3_ID:", nrow(bad_niche_assignments), "\n") # 22 
# View these rows 
bad_niche_assignments # there are 2 which are not needed, the rest are blanks for the NP so need to be relabelled to be BL

# Extract sample data
meta <- data.frame(VGO3_NPOP_dedup@sam_data)
meta$SampleID <- sample_names(VGO3_NPOP_dedup)
# Remove two specific samples by sample_identifier
samples_to_remove <- meta %>%
  filter(sample_identifier %in% c("ISO_38_OP_TOP_B1_M", "ISO_38_OP_TOP_B2_M")) %>%
  pull(SampleID)
# Prune to remove those samples
VGO3_NPOP_dedup <- prune_samples(!(sample_names(VGO3_NPOP_dedup) %in% samples_to_remove), VGO3_NPOP_dedup)
# Fix niche values where VGO3_ID is not a 5-digit number but niche is NP or OP
# Re-extract updated metadata
meta <- data.frame(VGO3_NPOP_dedup@sam_data)

# Identify the rows to correct
bad_niche_idx <- which(meta$niche %in% c("NP", "OP") & !grepl("^\\d{5}$", meta$VGO3_ID))

# Convert niche to character before assignment
meta$niche <- as.character(meta$niche)

# Now assign "FB" to the bad indices
meta$niche[bad_niche_idx] <- "FB"

# Optionally convert niche back to factor
meta$niche <- factor(meta$niche)

# Assign the modified metadata back to the phyloseq object
sample_data(VGO3_NPOP_dedup) <- sample_data(meta) 
table(VGO3_NPOP_dedup@sam_data$niche)

# How many samples have NA in 'niche' 
cat("Number of samples with NA in niche:", sum(is.na(meta$niche)), "\n") #3 have NA
# View the full rows where niche is still NA
meta_with_na_niche <- meta %>%
  filter(is.na(niche))
# Print or inspect these rows
print(meta_with_na_niche)
# Optional: view nicely in RStudio viewer
View(meta_with_na_niche)
# Extract metadata from the phyloseq object
meta <- data.frame(sample_data(VGO3_NPOP_dedup))
meta$SampleID <- sample_names(VGO3_NPOP_dedup)
# Assign "FB" to the two identified samples
fb_samples <- c("ISO_73_NP_TOP_BL_N5_1001", "ISO_73_NP_TOP_BL_N5_1005")
meta$niche[meta$sample_identifier %in% fb_samples] <- "FB"
# Identify the remaining samples that had NA in 'niche'
meta_with_na_niche <- meta %>%
  filter(is.na(niche) & !(sample_identifier %in% fb_samples))
# Set 'niche' to "unknown" for these
# Add "unknown" to the levels of niche if not already present
if (!"unknown" %in% levels(meta$niche)) {
  levels(meta$niche) <- c(levels(meta$niche), "unknown")
}
meta$niche[meta$SampleID %in% meta_with_na_niche$SampleID] <- "unknown"
# Update the phyloseq object with the revised metadata
sample_data(VGO3_NPOP_dedup) <- sample_data(meta)
# Optional: check that no NAs remain
cat("Remaining NAs in niche:", sum(is.na(sample_data(VGO3_NPOP_dedup)$niche)), "\n")


# Check remaining unique participants in NP and OP niches (population CP)
length(unique(VGO3_NPOP_dedup@sam_data$VGO3_ID[VGO3_NPOP_dedup@sam_data$niche == "NP" & VGO3_NPOP_dedup@sam_data$population == "CP"])) # 956
length(unique(VGO3_NPOP_dedup@sam_data$VGO3_ID[VGO3_NPOP_dedup@sam_data$niche == "OP" & VGO3_NPOP_dedup@sam_data$population == "CP"])) # 954

# Remove taxa with 0 sum
# VGO3_NPOP_dedup <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(VGO3_NPOP_dedup) > 0, VGO3_NPOP_dedup) # 2434  samples, 24134 taxa

# Add 'dataset' column to phyloseq object's sample_data
sample_data(VGO3_NPOP_dedup)$dataset <- "VGO3_NPOP"

# Add 'timepoint' column to each phyloseq object's sample_data
sample_data(VGO3_NPOP_dedup)$timepoint <- "T1"


# filter metadata in each dataset
names(VGO3_NPOP_dedup@sam_data) # keep: sample_identifier, DI.Run, qPCR, VGO3_ID,  dataset, niche, timepoint

# For VGO3_NPOP: keep DI.Run, qPCR, VGO3_ID, dataset
sample_data(VGO3_NPOP_dedup) <- sample_data(VGO3_NPOP_dedup)[, c("sample_identifier", "qPCR", "VGO3_ID", "dataset", "timepoint", "niche", "DI.Run")]

# rename some of the metadata columns to align with eachother for later merging
colnames(sample_data(VGO3_NPOP_dedup))[
  colnames(sample_data(VGO3_NPOP_dedup)) == "sample_identifier"
] <- "sample_ID"

```
# Save
```{r}
ps2 <- VGO3_NPOP_dedup
# Inspect
ps2@sam_data # Sample Data: [2362 samples by 61 sample variables]:

# Save ps 
saveRDS(ps2, "../Output/Cross_sectional/Phyloseq/ps_raw_VGO3.rds")
```
# Composition plots - before pre-processing
```{r}
# Subset ps2 for dataset == "VGO3_NP"
ps_vgo3_np <- subset_samples(ps2, dataset == "VGO3_NPOP" & niche == "NP")
ps_vgo3_np_filt <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(ps_vgo3_np) > 0, ps_vgo3_np)
ps_vgo3_np_filt <- prune_samples(sample_sums(ps_vgo3_np_filt) > 0, ps_vgo3_np_filt)

# stacked bar plot
VGO3_NP_stackedbar <- create_ordered_bar_n(ps_vgo3_np_filt, 15) + 
  theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(), 
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank()) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
  labs(title = "VGO3 NP microbiome composition per sample")
# Save plot
ggsave("../Output/Cross_sectional/Microbiome_descriptives/Before_preprocessing_composition/VGO3_NP_barplot.svg", VGO3_NP_stackedbar, width = 5, height = 6)


# Subset ps2 for dataset == "VGO3_NP"
ps_vgo3_op <- subset_samples(ps2, dataset == "VGO3_NPOP" & niche == "OP")
ps_vgo3_op_filt <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(ps_vgo3_op) > 0, ps_vgo3_op)
ps_vgo3_op_filt <- prune_samples(sample_sums(ps_vgo3_op_filt) > 0, ps_vgo3_op_filt)

# stacked bar plot
VGO3_OP_stackedbar <- create_ordered_bar_n(ps_vgo3_op_filt, 15) + 
  theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(), 
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank()) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
  labs(title = "VGO3 OP microbiome composition per sample")
# Save plot
ggsave("../Output/Cross_sectional/Microbiome_descriptives/Before_preprocessing_composition/VGO3_OP_barplot.svg", VGO3_OP_stackedbar, width = 5, height = 6)

```

# Pre-processing
## Visualise library size
```{r}
df <- meta_to_df(ps2)
df$LibrarySize <- sample_sums(ps2)
df <- df[order(df$LibrarySize),]
df$Index <- seq(nrow(df))

# Library sizes - all samples including blanks
libsize_niche <- ggplot(data = df, aes(x=Index, y=LibrarySize, color=niche)) + geom_point()
ggsave("../Output/Cross_sectional/Preprocessing/librarysize_perniche.svg", libsize_niche)

# Library size of NP and OP samples only 
df_filtered <- df %>% filter(niche %in% c("NP", "OP"))
libsize_niche_NPOP <- ggplot(data = df_filtered, aes(x = Index, y = LibrarySize, color = niche)) +
  geom_point()
ggsave("../Output/Cross_sectional/Preprocessing/librarysize_NPvsOP.svg", libsize_niche_NPOP, width = 6, height = 5)

```
## Visualise bacterial density 
```{r}
bacterial_density_NP_OP <- df %>%
  filter(niche %in% c("NP", "OP")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = niche, y = qPCR)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.4, fill = "white") +
  geom_jitter(aes(color = niche), width = 0.2, size = 1, alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(x = "Niche", y = "Bacterial density (pg/µl)", color = "Niche") +
  scale_y_continuous(trans = 'log10',
                     breaks = scales::trans_breaks('log10', function(x) 10^x),
                     labels = scales::trans_format('log10', scales::math_format(10^.x)))

ggsave("../Output/Preprocessing/bacterial_density_NPvsOP.svg", bacterial_density_NP_OP)

```
# Decontaminating the microbiome data
## Preparing data for decontamination
```{r}
VGO3_for_decontam <- ps2
table(VGO3_for_decontam@sam_data$niche) # BB, BD, BL, BP, FB, NP, OP, UMD, ZMCB, ZMCD
# For decontam we use the BD (Blank from DNA extraction) samples for the VGO3 dataset (NP and OP) (n=152)
sample_data(VGO3_for_decontam)$is_neg <- sample_data(VGO3_for_decontam)$niche == "BD"

# NP samples were in DI.Run = 39-74
# OP samples were in DI.Run = 1,2, 5-8, 10-12, 33-38, 75-91
## Therefore we only want to select the BD samples from these run to do decontam per niche

# Select BD samples for NP decontam
np_DI_runs <- 39:74

# Subset to NP and the correct BD runs
VGO3_NP_for_decontam <- subset_samples(
  VGO3_for_decontam,
  (niche == "NP") | (niche == "BD" & DI.Run %in% np_DI_runs)
)

# Prune taxa with 0 total counts
VGO3_NP_for_decontam <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(VGO3_NP_for_decontam) > 0, VGO3_NP_for_decontam)

# Define BD runs for OP
op_DI_runs <- c(1, 2, 5:8, 10:12, 33:38, 75:91)
# Subset to OP and the correct BD runs
VGO3_OP_for_decontam <- subset_samples(
  VGO3_for_decontam,
  (niche == "OP") | (niche == "BD" & DI.Run %in% op_DI_runs)
)
# Prune taxa with 0 total counts
VGO3_OP_for_decontam <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(VGO3_OP_for_decontam) > 0, VGO3_OP_for_decontam)

# Remove mocks & other unnecessary samples from the datasets
table(VGO3_NP_for_decontam@sam_data$niche) # BD 72, NP 957
table(VGO3_OP_for_decontam@sam_data$niche) # BD 37, OP 955

VGO3_NP_for_decontam <- subset_samples(VGO3_NP_for_decontam, niche != "BB") %>%  subset_samples(., niche != "BP") %>% subset_samples(., niche != "FB") %>% subset_samples(., niche != "UMD") %>%  subset_samples(., niche != "ZMCB") %>% subset_samples(., niche != "ZMCD") %>% subset_samples(., niche != "unknown") %>%  
  subset_samples(., qPCR != 0) # 1026  samples
table(VGO3_NP_for_decontam@sam_data$niche)

VGO3_OP_for_decontam <- subset_samples(VGO3_OP_for_decontam, niche != "BB") %>%  subset_samples(., niche != "BP") %>% subset_samples(., niche != "FB") %>% subset_samples(., niche != "UMD") %>%  subset_samples(., niche != "ZMCB") %>% subset_samples(., niche != "ZMCD") %>% subset_samples(., niche != "unknown") %>%  
  subset_samples(., qPCR != 0) # 991 samples
```
## Run decontam combined method
```{r}
rownames(VGO3_for_decontam@tax_table) # All ASV names

# VGO3 NP
# Prune taxa with total abundance of 0 across samples
VGO3_NP_for_decontam <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(VGO3_NP_for_decontam) > 0, VGO3_NP_for_decontam)
# Run decontam on NP subset (using method="combined")
contaminants_VGO3_NP <- isContaminant(VGO3_NP_for_decontam, method = "combined", conc = "qPCR", neg = "is_neg")
contaminants_VGO3_NP <- subset(contaminants_VGO3_NP, contaminant == TRUE)
dim(contaminants_VGO3_NP)[1] # 129 identified in the NP
write.csv(contaminants_VGO3_NP, "../Output/Cross_sectional/Decontamination/VGO3_NP_contaminants.csv")

# VGO3 OP
# Prune taxa with total abundance of 0 across samples
VGO3_OP_for_decontam <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(VGO3_OP_for_decontam) > 0, VGO3_OP_for_decontam)
# Run decontam on NP subset (using method="combined")
contaminants_VGO3_OP <- isContaminant(VGO3_OP_for_decontam, method = "combined", conc = "qPCR", neg = "is_neg")
contaminants_VGO3_OP <- subset(contaminants_VGO3_OP, contaminant == TRUE)
dim(contaminants_VGO3_OP)[1] # 88 identified in the OP
write.csv(contaminants_VGO3_OP, "../Output/Cross_sectional/Decontamination/VGO3_OP_contaminants.csv")
```
## Kruskall-Wallis across DNA isolation runs 
We implement a Kruskal-Wallis test to see which ASVs show discrepancies across the DNA isolation runs. These differences might indicate contamination, so the Kruskal-Wallis test is applied to each ASV to assess whether its relative abundance (RA) differs significantly across the DI.Run groups N.B. DI run groups are only available for VGO2 NP and VGO3 NP/OP dataset. 
```{r}
#### VGO3 NP ####
VGO3_NP_for_decontam_1 <- VGO3_NP_for_decontam %>% 
  pres_abund_filter() %>% # Start with taxa present in ≥2 samples at ≥0.1% RA 
  to_RA() %>% # convert to relative abundance
  pres_abund_filter(pres = 15, abund = 0.0001) # keep taxa in ≥15 samples at ≥0.01%

VGO3_NP_for_decontam_2 <- VGO3_NP_for_decontam %>% 
  pres_abund_filter() %>% #  Start with taxa present in ≥2 samples at ≥0.1% RA 
  to_RA() %>%  # convert to relative abundance
  pres_abund_filter(pres = 40, abund = 0.01) # keep taxa in ≥40 samples at ≥1%

VGO3_NP_for_decontam_1 <- prune_taxa(!taxa_names(VGO3_NP_for_decontam_1) %in% taxa_names(VGO3_NP_for_decontam_2), VGO3_NP_for_decontam_1) # Exclude the high-abundance/core taxa from ps_vgo3_np_for_decontam_1

# Reshape phyloseq 
df_VGO3_np <- ps_to_df(VGO3_NP_for_decontam_1)

# Identify ASVs from the phyloseq object
asv_names <- taxa_names(VGO3_NP_for_decontam_1)
# Keep only ASVs that actually exist in df_VGO2
existing_asvs <- intersect(asv_names, colnames(df_VGO3_np))

# Select DI.Run and ASV columns
df_VGO3_np <- df_VGO3_np %>%
  select(all_of(c("DI.Run", existing_asvs)))

# Pivot longer to long format (ASV, RA)
df_VGO3_np_pivot <- pivot_longer(df_VGO3_np, cols = existing_asvs,
                             names_to = "ASV", values_to = "RA")

# Run Kruskal-Wallis test per ASV
kruskal_results_vgo3_np <- df_VGO3_np_pivot %>%
  group_by(ASV) %>%
  summarise(
    kruskal_test = list(
      kruskal.test(RA ~ DI.Run, data = pick(DI.Run, RA)) %>% broom::tidy()
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  unnest(cols = kruskal_test)

# Flag significant ASVs as contaminants
kruskal_results_vgo3_np$contaminant <- ifelse(kruskal_results_vgo3_np$p.value < 0.001, TRUE, FALSE)
# Subset only significant (putative contaminants)
KW_contams_vgo3_np <- subset(kruskal_results_vgo3_np, contaminant == TRUE)
KW_contams_vgo3_np # 246 detected 
KW_contams_vgo3_np$ASV

# Filter for significant ASVs
KW_contams_VGO3NP <- ggplot(subset(df_VGO3_np_pivot, ASV %in% KW_contams_vgo3_np$ASV), 
            aes(x = as.character(DI.Run), y = RA)) +
  labs(y = "Relative abundance (%)", x = "DI Run") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, shape = 16, size = 2,
             position = position_jitter(width = 0.25, height = 0)) +
  geom_boxplot(colour = "black", alpha = 0.5) +
  stat_summary(fun = "mean", geom = "point", shape = 8,
               size = 2, color = "black") +
  facet_wrap(~ASV, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_bw()

ggsave("../Output/Cross_sectional/Decontamination/KW_contams_VGO3NP.png", KW_contams_VGO3NP, width = 25, height = 25)

#### VGO3 OP ####
VGO3_OP_for_decontam_1 <- VGO3_OP_for_decontam %>% 
  pres_abund_filter() %>% # Start with taxa present in ≥2 samples at ≥0.1% RA 
  to_RA() %>% # convert to relative abundance
  pres_abund_filter(pres = 15, abund = 0.0001) # keep taxa in ≥15 samples at ≥0.01%

VGO3_OP_for_decontam_2 <- VGO3_OP_for_decontam %>% 
  pres_abund_filter() %>% #  Start with taxa present in ≥2 samples at ≥0.1% RA 
  to_RA() %>%  # convert to relative abundance
  pres_abund_filter(pres = 40, abund = 0.01) # keep taxa in ≥40 samples at ≥1%

VGO3_OP_for_decontam_1 <- prune_taxa(!taxa_names(VGO3_OP_for_decontam_1) %in% taxa_names(VGO3_OP_for_decontam_2), VGO3_OP_for_decontam_1) # Exclude the high-abundance/core taxa from ps_vgo3_op_for_decontam_1

# Reshape phyloseq 
df_VGO3_op <- ps_to_df(VGO3_OP_for_decontam_1)

# Identify ASVs from the phyloseq object
asv_names <- taxa_names(VGO3_OP_for_decontam_1)
# Keep only ASVs that actually exist in df_VGO2
existing_asvs <- intersect(asv_names, colnames(df_VGO3_op))

# Select DI.Run and ASV columns
df_VGO3_op <- df_VGO3_op %>%
  select(all_of(c("DI.Run", existing_asvs)))

# Pivot longer to long format (ASV, RA)
df_VGO3_op_pivot <- pivot_longer(df_VGO3_op, cols = existing_asvs,
                                 names_to = "ASV", values_to = "RA")

# Run Kruskal-Wallis test per ASV
kruskal_results_vgo3_op <- df_VGO3_op_pivot %>%
  group_by(ASV) %>%
  summarise(
    kruskal_test = list(
      kruskal.test(RA ~ DI.Run, data = pick(DI.Run, RA)) %>% broom::tidy()
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  unnest(cols = kruskal_test)

# Flag significant ASVs as contaminants
kruskal_results_vgo3_op$contaminant <- ifelse(kruskal_results_vgo3_op$p.value < 0.001, TRUE, FALSE)
# Subset only significant (putative contaminants)
KW_contams_vgo3_op <- subset(kruskal_results_vgo3_op, contaminant == TRUE)
KW_contams_vgo3_op # 125 detected 
KW_contams_vgo3_op$ASV

# Filter for significant ASVs
KW_contams_VGO3OP <- ggplot(subset(df_VGO3_op_pivot, ASV %in% KW_contams_vgo3_op$ASV), 
                            aes(x = as.character(DI.Run), y = RA)) +
  labs(y = "Relative abundance (%)", x = "DI Run") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, shape = 16, size = 2,
             position = position_jitter(width = 0.25, height = 0)) +
  geom_boxplot(colour = "black", alpha = 0.5) +
  stat_summary(fun = "mean", geom = "point", shape = 8,
               size = 2, color = "black") +
  facet_wrap(~ASV, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_bw()

ggsave("../Output/Decontamination/Kruskall_Wallis/KW_contams_VGO3OP.png", KW_contams_VGO3OP, width = 20, height = 20)
```
## Combine contaminants from both methods per dataset
```{r}
# Check which contaminants are the same across decontam method and kruskall wallis
# Extract ASV names from decontam (rownames)
decontam_VGO3_NP_ASVs <- rownames(contaminants_VGO3_NP)
decontam_VGO3_OP_ASVs <- rownames(contaminants_VGO3_OP)

# Extract ASV names from Kruskal-Wallis results
KW_VGO3_NP_ASVs <- KW_contams_vgo3_np$ASV
KW_VGO3_OP_ASVs <- KW_contams_vgo3_op$ASV

# Function to get counts of overlaps and uniques
compare_contaminants <- function(decontam_asvs, kw_asvs) {
  decontam_set <- unique(decontam_asvs)
  kw_set <- unique(kw_asvs)
  
  shared <- intersect(decontam_set, kw_set)
  decontam_only <- setdiff(decontam_set, kw_set)
  kw_only <- setdiff(kw_set, decontam_set)
  
  list(
    shared_ASVs = shared,
    shared_count = length(shared),
    decontam_only_count = length(decontam_only),
    kw_only_count = length(kw_only)
  )
}

comparison_VGO3_NP <- compare_contaminants(decontam_VGO3_NP_ASVs, KW_VGO3_NP_ASVs)
comparison_VGO3_OP <- compare_contaminants(decontam_VGO3_OP_ASVs, KW_VGO3_OP_ASVs)

comparison_VGO3_NP
comparison_VGO3_OP

# Combine the contaminants identified by decontam with those detected using Kruskall-Wallis test
# VGO3_NP: both decontam and KW
contaminants_VGO3_NP_combined <- unique(c(decontam_VGO3_NP_ASVs, KW_VGO3_NP_ASVs))
length(contaminants_VGO3_NP_combined) # 357
# VGO3_OP: both decontam and KW
contaminants_VGO3_OP_combined <- unique(c(decontam_VGO3_OP_ASVs, KW_VGO3_OP_ASVs))
length(contaminants_VGO3_OP_combined)# 187 

# Sort ASVs in the list by their ranking (i.e. the number suffix at the end of their name)
sort_asvs_by_index <- function(asvs) {
  index <- as.numeric(sub(".*_", "", asvs))  # extract suffix
  asvs[order(index)]
}

# VGO3 NP
contaminants_VGO3_NP_combined_sorted <- sort_asvs_by_index(contaminants_VGO3_NP_combined)
# VGO3 OP
contaminants_VGO3_OP_combined_sorted <- sort_asvs_by_index(contaminants_VGO3_OP_combined)

# Create table of contaminants detected by each method and in each dataset 
# Create a union of all contaminant ASVs from all datasets
all_contaminants <- unique(c(
  contaminants_VGO3_NP_combined_sorted,
  contaminants_VGO3_OP_combined_sorted
))

length(all_contaminants) # 486

# Initialize data frame
final_contaminant_df <- data.frame(
  ASV = all_contaminants,
  VGO3_NP_decontam = all_contaminants %in% contaminants_VGO3_NP_combined_sorted,
  VGO3_OP_decontam = all_contaminants %in% contaminants_VGO3_OP_combined_sorted
)

# Optional: sort by numeric ASV suffix for readability
final_contaminant_df <- final_contaminant_df[order(as.numeric(sub(".*_", "", final_contaminant_df$ASV))), ]

# View final table
head(final_contaminant_df)

# Write to CSV
write.csv(final_contaminant_df, "../Output/Cross_sectional/Decontamination/Final_contaminants_combined.csv", row.names = FALSE)
# Now to do manual checks of ASVs flagged as contaminants
```
## Frequency plot of contaminants
We create a frequency plot of the top 100 identified contaminants. For most of these contaminant ASVs there seems to be a correlation between their relative abundance and the bacterial density (for VGO2 OP the pattern isn't so consistent)
```{r}
# Select top 100 ASVs (or fewer if < 100 available)
top100_VGO3_NP <- head(contaminants_VGO3_NP_combined_sorted, 100)
top100_VGO3_OP <- head(contaminants_VGO3_OP_combined_sorted, 100)

# Frequency plots
# VGO3 NP
plot_VGO3_NP <- plot_frequency(subset_samples(VGO3_NP_for_decontam, niche == "NP"), top100_VGO3_NP, 
               conc="qPCR") +  xlab("Bacterial density (pg/ul)")

# VGO3 OP
plot_VGO3_OP <- plot_frequency(subset_samples(VGO3_OP_for_decontam, niche == "OP"), top100_VGO3_OP, 
               conc="qPCR") +  xlab("Bacterial density (pg/ul)")

# Save plots
ggsave("../Output/Cross_sectional/Decontamination/freqplot_VGO3_NP_top100.svg", plot_VGO3_NP, width=15, height=15)
ggsave("../Output/Cross_sectional/Decontamination/freqplot_VGO3_OP_top100.svg", plot_VGO3_OP, width=15, height=15)

# x-axis: Bacterial load (e.g., qPCR concentration, here pg/μl)
# y-axis: Frequency (relative abundance) of an individual ASV

## For true contaminants we expect higher relative abundance at lower bacterial loads, and a declining or flat trend as load increases. There should be a negative correlation between ASV abundance and sample bacterial density. If it is a false contaminant, then there will be no clear correlation with bacterial load. 
```
## Differences in contaminants between niches 
```{r}
# Identify contaminants shared between 
# Compare NP vs OP for VGO2
compare_np_op <- function(np_asvs, op_asvs) {
  shared <- intersect(np_asvs, op_asvs)
  np_only <- setdiff(np_asvs, op_asvs)
  op_only <- setdiff(op_asvs, np_asvs)
  
  list(
    shared_ASVs = shared,
    shared_count = length(shared),
    np_only_ASVs = np_only,
    np_only_count = length(np_only),
    op_only_ASVs = op_only,
    op_only_count = length(op_only)
  )
}

# VGO3: Compare contaminants between NP and OP
np_op_comparison_VGO3 <- compare_np_op(
  contaminants_VGO3_NP_combined_sorted,
  contaminants_VGO3_OP_combined_sorted
)

# View the results
np_op_comparison_VGO3 # 58 shared, 299 NP only, 129 OP only 

```
## Check false contaminants
After screening the list of combined contaminants, we found some that some ASVs may not actually be contaminants.
```{r}
# Specifically we see that there are some high abundant common NP ASVs that are flagged as contaminants in the VGO3 OP dataset. But in the VGO3 sequencing, there were some mixed seuqencing runs (i.e. mixing NP and OP niches). Checking in which runs these common NP ASVs appear to see if we can see signs of spill-over from the NP to OP niches.

# Common NP contaminants to check
# Define the ASVs you want to check for spill-over
contams_check_vgo3_OP <- c("Dolosigranulum_pigrum_1", "Staphylococcus_2", "Corynebacterium_3", "Moraxella_4")
# Filter to relevant ASVs in VGO3 OP and keep only non-zero abundance
df_vgo3_op_contams_present <- df_VGO3_op_pivot %>%
  filter(ASV %in% contams_check_vgo3_OP, RA > 0)
# For each ASV, list the unique DI.Runs where it's present
asv_run_presence_simple <- df_vgo3_op_contams_present %>%
  group_by(ASV) %>%
  summarise(
    Runs_present = paste(sort(unique(DI.Run)), collapse = ", "),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
# View result
print(asv_run_presence_simple)
# Optionally write to file
write.csv(asv_run_presence_simple, "../Output/Decontamination/VGO3_OP_suspect_ASV_run_presence.csv", row.names = FALSE) # The ASVs seem to be present across a selection of different DNA isolation runs - therefore not probably related to spill-over from NP niche

# Extra check: 
# Check the relative abundance across DNA isolation runs for these suspicious contaminants identified. 
# Define ASVs of interest
asvs_to_check <- c("Dolosigranulum_pigrum_1", "Staphylococcus_2", "Corynebacterium_3", "Moraxella_4")
# Filter dataset for ASVs of interest and ensure DI.Run is ordered
plot_data <- df_VGO3_op_pivot %>%
  filter(ASV %in% asvs_to_check) %>%
  mutate(DI.Run = factor(DI.Run, levels = sort(unique(as.numeric(as.character(DI.Run))))))
# Plot
p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = DI.Run, y = RA)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, shape = 16, size = 2, position = position_jitter(width = 0.2)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, fill = "grey90", alpha = 0.4) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", shape = 8, size = 2, color = "red") +
  facet_wrap(~ASV, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    x = "DNA Isolation Run (DI.Run)",
    y = "Relative Abundance",
    title = "RA of suspect ASVs across runs (VGO3 OP)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1)
  )
# Display plot
print(p)
# Save plot 
ggsave("../Output/Cross_sectional/Decontamination/RA_by_run_VGO3_OP_suspects.svg", p, width = 10, height = 6)# no clear pattern

```
## Non-contaminants after manual inspection
```{r}
# Define non-contaminants manually verified
non_contam <- c(
  "Dolosigranulum_pigrum_1", "Staphylococcus_2", "Corynebacterium_3",
  "Moraxella_4", "Veillonella_140", "Prevotella_7_melaninogenica_165",
  "Anaerococcus_provencensis_168", "Acinetobacter_lwoffii_177", "Prevotella_7_179",
  "Cutibacterium_187", "Lactococcus_209", "Actinomyces_225"
)

# Filter contaminants by removing known non-contaminants
all_contaminants_filt <- setdiff(all_contaminants, non_contam)
cat("Number of contaminants after filtering non-contaminants:", length(all_contaminants_filt), "\n") # 483 
```
## Exclude all blanks from phyloseq objects 
```{r}
# Subset phyloseq object to samples from niches "NP" and "OP"
ps2_NPOP <- subset_samples(ps2, niche %in% c("NP", "OP"))

# Remove taxa with zero counts after subsetting samples
ps2_NPOP_f <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(ps2_NPOP) > 0, ps2_NPOP)
ps2_NPOP; ps2_NPOP_f # 6710 taxa removed
```
## Remove contaminants
```{r}
# Remove contaminant taxa from the filtered phyloseq object
ps2_NPOP_no_contams <- prune_taxa(!(taxa_names(ps2_NPOP_f) %in% all_contaminants_filt), ps2_NPOP_f)

cat("Contaminants removed:", length(taxa_names(ps2_NPOP_f)) - length(taxa_names(ps2_NPOP_no_contams)), "\n") # 483 removed 

# Remove any taxa with zero counts after contaminant removal
ps2_no_zero_taxa <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(ps2_NPOP_no_contams) > 0, ps2_NPOP_no_contams)

# Final object: ps2_no_zero_taxa contains no contaminants and no zero-count taxa
```
# Final list of ASVs remaining
```{r}
# Extract ASV names from phyloseq object
asv_names <- taxa_names(ps2_no_zero_taxa)
# Extract numeric suffix (everything after last underscore)
asv_suffix_numbers <- as.numeric(sub(".*_", "", asv_names))
# Order ASVs by numeric suffix
asv_ordered <- asv_names[order(asv_suffix_numbers)]
# Take only the first 100
asv_first_100 <- head(asv_ordered, 100)
# Print
print(asv_first_100)
# save final ASV list
write.csv(asv_first_100, "../Output/Cross_sectional/Microbiome_descriptives/Ordered_ASV_names_top100.csv")
```

# Check for duplicated samples
```{r}
# Duplicates were already resolved before but check if any were missed
# Convert sample_data to data.frame
sample_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps2_no_zero_taxa))
# Create a new combined key of VGO3_ID and niche
sample_df$combined_key <- paste(sample_df$VGO3_ID, sample_df$niche, sample_df$study, sep = "_")
# Count how many times each combined_key appears
counts <- table(sample_df$combined_key)
# Keep keys that appear more than once (true duplicates)
dup_keys <- names(counts[counts > 1]) # None
```
# Exclude low density samples
Next, we remove all the samples with low bacterial density (lower than 0.095 pg/ul). This includes 20 samples
```{r}
ps3 <- prune_samples(sample_data(ps2_no_zero_taxa)$qPCR >= 0.095, ps2_no_zero_taxa)
ps3 <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(ps3) > 0, ps3) # Remove taxa with taxa sum of 0

ps2_no_zero_taxa; ps3 # 11 samples were removed 
# phyloseq-class experiment-level object
# otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 18446 taxa and 1912 samples ]
# sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 1912 samples by 61 sample variables ]
# tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 18446 taxa by 9 taxonomic ranks ]
# refseq()      DNAStringSet:      [ 18446 reference sequences ]
# phyloseq-class experiment-level object
# otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 18288 taxa and 1901 samples ]
# sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 1901 samples by 61 sample variables ]
# tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 18288 taxa by 9 taxonomic ranks ]
# refseq()      DNAStringSet:      [ 18288 reference sequences ]


```
# Rarefaction curves
```{r}
# First we take a look at the distribution of # reads per sample
read_counts <- sample_sums(ps3) %>% enframe() %>% ggplot(aes(y = 1, x = value)) +
  ggdist::stat_halfeye( adjust = .5, height = .3, justification = -.3,
                        .width = 0, point_colour = NA) + 
  geom_point(size = 1.3, shape = 16, alpha = .2, 
             position = position_jitter(seed = 1, width = 0, height = .05)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = .12, alpha = 0.5, outlier.color = NA) +
  scale_x_log10(breaks = c(1000, 5000, 10000, 30000, 100000, 300000),
                labels = scales::comma) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0.9, NA), clip = "off") +
  labs(x = "Number of reads/sample", y = NULL) +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_blank()) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 10000, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
read_counts
ggsave("../Output/Cross_sectional/Preprocessing/read_counts.svg", read_counts, width = 5, height = 4)

# Rarefaction curves - helps evaluate whether the sequencing depth (i.e., number of reads per sample) was sufficient to capture the diversity of microbial communities in the samples.
# X-axis: Number of sequencing reads (sampling effort)
# Y-axis: Number of observed species (or ASVs/OTUs, depending on your level of analysis)
## Each line on the curve represents a sample.
otu_tab_rare <- ps3 %>% prune_samples(sample_sums(.) < 30000, .) %>% # only keep samples with <30000 reads
  otu_table() %>% # extract otu table
  as(., "matrix") %>% # convert it to a matrix
  t # transpose the matrix where rows = samples and cols = taxa

saveRDS(otu_tab_rare, "../Output/Cross_sectional/Preprocessing/otu_tab_rare.rds")


# Generate rarefaction curves
# rarecurve() (from the vegan package) performs individual-based rarefaction for each sample by randomly subsampling reads without replacement at increasing steps (every 10 reads, here) and calculating the number of unique taxa detected at each step.
rare_curve_data <- rarecurve(otu_tab_rare, step = 10)

rare_curves <- map2_dfr(rare_curve_data,
                       rownames(otu_tab_rare), ~enframe(.x) %>%
                           mutate(sample_id = .y)) %>%
   mutate(name = str_remove(name, "N") %>% as.numeric) %>%
   ggplot(aes(x = name, y = value, group = sample_id)) +
     geom_line(alpha = 0.15) +
     scale_y_log10() +
     scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0, 5000, 10000, 15000, 20000, 25000),
                        labels = scales::comma) +
     geom_vline(aes(xintercept = 10000),  colour="grey50", linetype="dotted") +
     labs(x = "Sample size", y = "Number of species")

 ggsave("../Output/Cross_sectional/Microbiome_descriptives/rarefaction_curves.svg", rare_curves)
```

# Exclusion of samples with less than 10,000 reads
```{r}
# Based on these figures, we chose a read cut-off at 10,000 reads. In the rarefaction curves, this was the point where the curves started flattening. 
# Remove the samples with <10,000 reads
ps4 <- prune_samples(sample_sums(ps3) > 10000, ps3)
ps4 <- prune_samples(!is.na(ps4@sam_data$niche), ps4)
ps4 <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(ps4) > 0, ps4)

ps3; ps4

# phyloseq-class experiment-level object
# otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 18288 taxa and 1901 samples ]
# sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 1901 samples by 61 sample variables ]
# tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 18288 taxa by 9 taxonomic ranks ]
# refseq()      DNAStringSet:      [ 18288 reference sequences ]
# phyloseq-class experiment-level object
# otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 16030 taxa and 1692 samples ]
# sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 1692 samples by 61 sample variables ]
# tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 16030 taxa by 9 taxonomic ranks ]
# refseq()      DNAStringSet:      [ 16030 reference sequences ]
```

```{r}
ps_vgo3_np <- subset_samples(ps4, dataset == "VGO3_NPOP" & niche == "NP")
ps_vgo3_np_filt <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(ps_vgo3_np) > 0, ps_vgo3_np)
ps_vgo3_np_filt <- prune_samples(sample_sums(ps_vgo3_np_filt) > 0, ps_vgo3_np_filt)

ps_vgo3_op_filt <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(ps_vgo3_op) > 0, ps_vgo3_op)
ps_vgo3_op_filt <- prune_samples(sample_sums(ps_vgo3_op_filt) > 0, ps_vgo3_op_filt)
```

# Composition plots - average RA per dataset (genus level)
Make plots which collapse ASVs to genera, calculate mean relative abundances for each genus per dataset, and keep genera that have >10% mean RA, create side-by-side bar plot across the datasets

# Rarefy data and compute alpha diversity indices 
```{r}
m <- ps4 %>% meta_to_df()
gc()
set.seed(77)
ps_rarefied <- phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth(ps4, replace = T, trimOTUs = T)
sample_sums(ps_rarefied) # min library size = 10038    

# 2638OTUs were removed because they are no longer present in any sample after random subsampling
# Compute alpha diversity indices: Observed, shannon and simpson
adiv <- data.frame(
  "Observed" = phyloseq::estimate_richness(ps_rarefied, measures = "Observed"),
  "Shannon" = phyloseq::estimate_richness(ps_rarefied, measures = "Shannon"),
  "Simpson" = phyloseq::estimate_richness(ps_rarefied, measures = "Simpson")
  )
# sample id have been changed to "." instead of "_"
adiv$sample_id <- gsub("\\.", "-", rownames(adiv))
m <- dplyr::left_join(m, adiv)
m$qPCR_log <- log2(m$qPCR)

# Add alpha diversity values to phyloseq object
rownames(m) <- m$sample_id
m <- select(m, -c("sample_id"))
sample_data(ps4) <- m

# Add read number metadata
ps4@sam_data$final_read_nr <- colSums(ps4@otu_table)
```

```{r}
stats <- tibble(step = character(),
                samples = numeric(),
                taxa = numeric(),
                reads = numeric())


stats <- ps_stats(ps, stats, "Raw data")
stats <- ps_stats(ps2_NPOP_f, stats, "Removal of blanks and zero sum taxa")
stats <- ps_stats(ps2_no_zero_taxa, stats, "Exclusion of contaminant ASVs")
stats <- ps_stats(ps3, stats, "Exclusion of samples with bacterial density <0.095 pg/uL")
stats <- ps_stats(ps4, stats, "Exclusion of samples with reads <10,000")
stats <- ps_stats(ps_rarefied, stats, "Rarefied for alpha diversity")
stats

write.csv(stats, "../Output/Cross_sectional/Preprocessing/Processing_steps.csv")

```
# Number samples in each dataset before and after pre-processing
```{r}
# Define a helper function to count samples
count_samples <- function(ps_obj, niche_val, dataset_val) {
  sample_data(ps_obj) %>%
    data.frame() %>%
    filter(niche == niche_val, dataset == dataset_val) %>%
    nrow()
}

# Create the summary table
sample_counts <- tibble(
  Condition = c("NP - VGO3_OPNP", "OP - VGO3_NPOP"),
  
  Before = c(
    count_samples(ps2_NPOP_f, "NP", "VGO3_NPOP"),
    count_samples(ps2_NPOP_f, "OP", "VGO3_NPOP")
  ),
  
  After = c(
    count_samples(ps4, "NP", "VGO3_NPOP"),
    count_samples(ps4, "OP", "VGO3_NPOP")
  )
)

# View the table
print(sample_counts)

write.csv(sample_counts, "../Output/Cross_sectional/Preprocessing/Sample_counts_before_after_preprocessing.csv")
```
# Metadata additions and fixes 
## Ensure variables are correct format
```{r}
ps4@sam_data # only metadata from sequencing

# Load participant metadata
metadata_VGO3 <- read.csv("../Output/Participant_data/Output_datasets/VGO3_data.csv")

# Convert ID to VGO3_ID
colnames(metadata_VGO3)[colnames(metadata_VGO3) == "ID"] <- "VGO3_ID"

# Extract sample_data from phyloseq
ps4_meta <- data.frame(sample_data(ps4))
ps4_meta$SampleName <- rownames(ps4_meta)  # keep rownames for reassignment

# Match correct metadata to each sample based on ID and timepoint
ps_meta_joined <- ps4_meta %>%
  mutate(VGO3_ID = as.character(VGO3_ID)) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(
    metadata_VGO3 %>% mutate(VGO3_ID = as.character(VGO3_ID)),
    by = c("VGO3_ID")
  )

# Remove column X from dataframe
ps_meta_joined$X <- NULL

str(ps_meta_joined)

# Correct metadata variable data structure
ps_meta_joined <- ps_meta_joined %>%
  mutate(
    Date = ymd(Date),
    Gender = as.factor(Gender),
    Age = as.numeric(Age),
    Ethnicity = as.character(Ethnicity),
    Spirometrist = as.character(Spirometrist),
    Gender = as.factor(Gender),
    Height = as.numeric(Height),
    Weight = as.numeric(Weight),
    BMI = as.numeric(BMI),
    Education_level = as.factor(Education_level),
    Smoking_status = as.factor(Smoking_status),
    Pneumonia_lastyr = as.factor(Pneumonia_lastyr),
    COPD_emphy_doc_diag = as.factor(COPD_emphy_doc_diag),
    Asthma_current = as.factor(Asthma_current),
    Asthma_ever = as.factor(Asthma_ever),
    Asthma_doc_diag = as.factor(Asthma_doc_diag),
    Covid_lastmonth = as.factor(Covid_lastmonth),
    Asthma_attk_lastyr = as.factor(Asthma_attk_lastyr),
    Lung_med = as.factor(Lung_med),
    Atopic = as.factor(Atopic),
    Asthma_med_current = as.factor(Asthma_med_current),
    Cortinhal_lastyr = as.factor(Cortinhal_lastyr),
    Antibiotics_lastmonth = as.factor(Antibiotics_lastmonth),
    Antibiotics_lastyr = as.factor(Antibiotics_lastyr),
    Farm_childhood = as.factor(Farm_childhood),
    Farm_visit_lastyr = as.factor(Farm_visit_lastyr),
    T0_wkprior_NH3 = as.numeric(T0_wkprior_NH3),
    T1_wkprior_NH3 = as.numeric(T1_wkprior_NH3),
    T1_T0_NH3_diff = as.numeric(T1_T0_NH3_diff),
    FEV1 = as.numeric(FEV1),
    FVC = as.numeric(FVC),
    FEV1_FVC = as.numeric(FEV1_FVC),
    MMEF = as.numeric(MMEF),
    FEV1_pred = as.numeric(FEV1_pred),
    FVC_pred = as.numeric(FVC_pred),
    FEV1FVC_pred = as.numeric(FEV1FVC_pred),
    MMEF_pred = as.numeric(MMEF_pred))

str(ps_meta_joined)

# Replace sample_data with updated metadata (ps_meta_joined)
ps4_m <- ps4
rownames(ps_meta_joined) <- ps_meta_joined$SampleName
sample_data(ps4_m) <- sample_data(ps_meta_joined)

```

## Categorise FEV1/FVC and add into sample data of ps4_m
```{r}
## Categorise obstruction from FEV1/FVC only
# Helper to add just the obstruction category
categorise_lung_function <- function(df) {
  # ensure numeric
  fev1fvc_num <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(df$FEV1_FVC)))
  df$FEV1FVC_category <- cut(
    fev1fvc_num,
    breaks = c(-Inf, 0.70, Inf),
    labels = c("Obstructive", "Normal"),
    right = FALSE
  )
  df$FEV1FVC_category <- factor(df$FEV1FVC_category,
                                levels = c("Normal","Obstructive"))
  df
}


# Add only this category to the phyloseq object
add_lf_categories_to_ps <- function(ps) {
  sd <- as(phyloseq::sample_data(ps), "data.frame")
  # make sure the raw ratio is numeric
  if ("FEV1_FVC" %in% names(sd)) {
    sd$FEV1_FVC <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(sd$FEV1_FVC)))
  }
  sd <- categorise_lung_function(sd)
  phyloseq::sample_data(ps) <- sd
  ps
}

# Apply
ps4_m <- add_lf_categories_to_ps(ps4_m)


# Visually look at categories of FEV1/FVC obstruction
sd <- as(phyloseq::sample_data(ps4_m), "data.frame")

# Clean facet label (unchanged logic)
sd$Dataset <- dplyr::case_when(
  sd$dataset %in% c("VGO2_NP","VGO2_OP") ~ sd$dataset,
  sd$dataset == "VGO3_NPOP" ~ paste0("VGO3_", sd$niche),
  TRUE ~ paste(sd$dataset, sd$niche, sep = "_")
)

lung_cat_vars <- c("FEV1FVC_category")  # only this one

lung_cat_counts <- sd %>%
  dplyr::select(any_of(c("Dataset", lung_cat_vars))) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(across(all_of(lung_cat_vars), ~ as.character(.))) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(lung_cat_vars),
    names_to = "Variable",
    values_to = "Category",
    values_drop_na = TRUE
  ) %>%
  dplyr::count(Dataset, Variable, Category, name = "n") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Variable = "FEV1/FVC",
    Category = factor(Category, levels = c("Normal","Obstructive"))
  )

cat_cols <- c(
  "Normal"      = "#2B83BA",
  "Obstructive" = "#762A83"
)

p_counts <- ggplot(lung_cat_counts, aes(x = Variable, y = n, fill = Category)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.85) +
  facet_wrap(~ Dataset, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cat_cols, drop = TRUE) +
  labs(
    title = "FEV1/FVC Obstruction Category per Dataset",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Number of Samples",
    fill = "Category"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5)
  )

p_counts

ggsave("../Output/Participant_data/Lung_function_obstruction_barplot.svg",
       p_counts, width = 8, height = 6)

```
# Add season to phyloseq metadata
```{r}
# take sample_data out as a data frame
sd <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps4_m))

# pick the date column you trust
# simple month → season mapper
get_season <- function(mo) {
  ifelse(mo %in% 3:5,  "Spring",
  ifelse(mo %in% 6:8,  "Summer",
  ifelse(mo %in% 9:11, "Autumn", "Winter")))
}

sd$season_microbiome_sampling <- get_season(month(sd$Date))

# make it a factor (nice for plotting later)
sd$season_microbiome_sampling <- factor(
  sd$season_microbiome_sampling,
  levels = c("Winter", "Spring", "Summer", "Autumn")
)

# write it back into the phyloseq
sample_data(ps4_m) <- sd
```
# Save phyloseq
```{r}
saveRDS(ps4_m, "../Output/Cross_sectional/Phyloseq/ps_final_VGO3.rds")
```

```{r}
sample_data(ps_rarefied) <- meta_ps4_m

saveRDS(ps_rarefied, "../Output/Cross_sectional/Phyloseq/ps_final_rarefied_VGO3.rds")
```

# Population summary table
```{r}
# Add the variable RespDx to the sam_data 
make_respdx <- function(meta) {
  to_yesno <- function(x) dplyr::case_when(
    x %in% c(1,"1","yes","Yes","YES",TRUE) ~ "yes",
    x %in% c(0,"0","no","No","NO",FALSE)   ~ "no",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  )
  a <- to_yesno(meta$Asthma_current)
  c <- to_yesno(meta$COPD_emphy_doc_diag)
  y <- ifelse(!is.na(a) & a=="yes" | !is.na(c) & c=="yes", "yes", "no")
  factor(y, levels = c("no","yes"))
}

# - Apply to ps_final_VGO3 and save
sd <- as(phyloseq::sample_data(ps4_m), "data.frame")
phyloseq::sample_data(ps4_m)$RespDx <- make_respdx(sd)

# Pull sample_data and keep only NP/OP samples
sd <- sample_data(ps4_m) %>%
  data.frame(check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%
  filter(niche %in% c("NP","OP"))

sd_vgo3 <- sd %>%
  mutate(niche_rank = ifelse(niche == "NP", 1L, 2L)) %>%   # prefer NP (1) over OP (2)
  arrange(VGO3_ID, niche_rank) %>%                         # put NP first within each ID
  filter(!duplicated(VGO3_ID)) %>%                         # keep first row per VGO3_ID
  select(-niche_rank)

## Just select the variables for Table 1
length(sd_vgo3)
df_vgo3 <- sd_vgo3 %>%
  select(
    Age, Gender, Height, Weight, BMI, Ethnicity, Education_level,RespDx,
    Lung_med, Asthma_med_current, Antibiotics_lastmonth, Antibiotics_lastyr, Pneumonia_lastyr, Asthma_ever, Asthma_doc_diag, Asthma_attk_lastyr, COPD_emphy_doc_diag,
    Smoking_status, Farm_childhood, Farm_visit_lastyr, T0_wkprior_NH3, T1_wkprior_NH3, FEV1, FVC, FEV1_FVC, MMEF, FEV1_pred, FVC_pred, FEV1FVC_pred, MMEF_pred
  )

# Helper functions for summary
cont_summary <- function(x) {
  x <- x[!is.na(x)]
  if (!length(x)) return(NA_character_)
  m  <- mean(x)
  s  <- sd(x)
  q  <- stats::quantile(x, probs = c(.25,.5,.75), na.rm = TRUE)
  sprintf("%.1f (%.1f)\n%.1f [%.1f, %.1f]",
          m, s, q[2], q[1], q[3])
}

cat_summary <- function(x) {
  x <- factor(x)
  if (all(is.na(x))) return(NA_character_)
  n_tot <- sum(!is.na(x))
  levs <- levels(x)
  out <- sapply(levs, function(lv) {
    n <- sum(x == lv, na.rm = TRUE)
    pct <- if (n_tot > 0) 100 * n / n_tot else NA_real_
    sprintf("%s: %d (%.1f%%)", lv, n, pct)
  }, USE.NAMES = FALSE)
  paste(out, collapse = "\n")
}



## Build the Table 1 rows in the order you want them to appear
rows_vgo3 <- tibble::tibble(
  Variable = c(
    "Age", "Gender", "Height", "Weight", "BMI", "Ethnicity", "RespDx", "Education_level",
    "Lung_med", "Asthma_med_current", "Antibiotics_lastmonth", "Antibiotics_lastyr","Pneumonia_lastyr", "Asthma_ever", "Asthma_doc_diag", "Asthma_attk_lastyr", "COPD_emphy_doc_diag",
    "Smoking_status", "Farm_childhood", "Farm_visit_lastyr","T0_wkprior_NH3", "T1_wkprior_NH3","FEV1", "FVC", "FEV1_FVC", "FEF25_75", 
    "FEV1_pred", "FVC_pred", "FEV1FVC_pred", "FEF25_75_pred"
  ),
  `VGO3` = c(
    cont_summary(df_vgo3$Age),
    cat_summary(df_vgo3$Gender),
    cont_summary(df_vgo3$Height),
    cont_summary(df_vgo3$Weight),
    cont_summary(df_vgo3$BMI),
    cat_summary(df_vgo3$Ethnicity),
    cat_summary(df_vgo3$RespDx),
    cat_summary(df_vgo3$Education_level),
    cat_summary(df_vgo3$Lung_med),
    cat_summary(df_vgo3$Asthma_med_current),
    cat_summary(df_vgo3$Antibiotics_lastmonth),
    cat_summary(df_vgo3$Antibiotics_lastyr),
    cat_summary(df_vgo3$Pneumonia_lastyr),
    cat_summary(df_vgo3$Asthma_ever),
    cat_summary(df_vgo3$Asthma_doc_diag),
    cat_summary(df_vgo3$Asthma_attk_lastyr),
    cat_summary(df_vgo3$COPD_emphy_doc_diag),
    cat_summary(df_vgo3$Smoking_status),
    cat_summary(df_vgo3$Farm_childhood),
    cat_summary(df_vgo3$Farm_visit_lastyr),
    cont_summary(df_vgo3$T0_wkprior_NH3),
    cont_summary(df_vgo3$T1_wkprior_NH3),
    cont_summary(df_vgo3$FEV1),
    cont_summary(df_vgo3$FVC),
    cont_summary(df_vgo3$FEV1_FVC),
    cont_summary(df_vgo3$MMEF),
    cont_summary(df_vgo3$FEV1_pred),
    cont_summary(df_vgo3$FVC_pred),
    cont_summary(df_vgo3$FEV1FVC_pred),
    cont_summary(df_vgo3$MMEF_pred)
  )
)

# Write to Excel
writexl::write_xlsx(
  list("Table 1 - VGO3" = rows_vgo3),
  path = "../Output/Cross_sectional/Participant_data/Table1_VGO3.xlsx"
)

median(sd_vgo3$Observed)
```

# ANALYSES
Firstly we look at the relationship between the microbiome and lung function cross-sectionally in 2021/22 (VGO3)
# Data 
```{r}
ps_final_VGO3 <- ps4_m
# phyloseq-class experiment-level object
# otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 16030 taxa and 1692 samples ]
# sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 1692 samples by 53 sample variables ]
# tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 16030 taxa by 9 taxonomic ranks ]
# refseq()      DNAStringSet:      [ 16030 reference sequences ]

sum(ps_final_VGO3@sam_data$niche=="NP")
sum(ps_final_VGO3@sam_data$niche=="OP")

# Count number of ASVs, phyla, classes, orders, families, genera, species for the entire dataset
# total ASVs
n_asv <- ntaxa(ps_final_VGO3) # 16030 taxa, 1692 samples

# helper to count distinct non-empty taxa at a rank (case-insensitive match)
count_rank <- function(ps, rank_name) {
  tt <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ps), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  col <- grep(paste0("^", rank_name, "$"), colnames(tt), ignore.case = TRUE, value = TRUE)
  if (length(col) == 0) return(NA_integer_)
  x <- tt[[col[1]]]
  x <- x[!is.na(x) & nzchar(x) & !x %in% c("NA", "Unassigned", "unclassified", "unknown")]
  length(unique(x))
}

ranks <- c("Phylum","Class","Order","Family","Genus","Species")
tax_counts <- tibble::tibble(
  level = ranks,
  n = vapply(ranks, function(r) count_rank(ps_final_VGO3, r), integer(1))
)

# show results
tax_counts
# Save 
write_xlsx(tax_counts, "../Output/Cross_sectional/Microbiome_descriptives/After_preprocessing_composition/Taxonomy_counts.xlsx")
```
# Separate microbiome data per dataset and niche
```{r}
# Subset per niche
# VGO3_NPOP dataset, NP niche
microbiome_VGO3_NP <- subset_samples(ps_final_VGO3, niche == "NP")
nsamples(microbiome_VGO3_NP) # 740 NP samples

# VGO3_NPOP dataset, OP niche
microbiome_VGO3_OP <- subset_samples(ps_final_VGO3, niche == "OP")
nsamples(microbiome_VGO3_OP) # 952 OP samples

# VGO3_NPOP dataset, NP & OP niches together
microbiome_VGO3_NPOP <- subset_samples(ps_final_VGO3, niche = c("OP", "NP"))
nsamples(microbiome_VGO3_NPOP) # 1692 OP samples
```
# Filter taxa and convert to relative abundance
```{r}
# VGO3 NP 
# Remove low abundant ASVs and convert counts to relative abundances
ps_vgo3_np_filtered <- microbiome_VGO3_NP %>% 
  pres_abund_filter() %>% # 0.1% relative abundance in at least 2 samples is default
  to_RA()
# A total of 882 ASVs were found to be present at or above a level of confident detection (0.1% relative abundance) in at least 2 samples (n = 15148 ASVs excluded).

# VGO3 OP 
# Remove low abundant ASVs and convert counts to relative abundances
ps_vgo3_op_filtered <- microbiome_VGO3_OP %>% 
  pres_abund_filter() %>% 
  to_RA()
# A total of 1087 ASVs were found to be present at or above a level of confident detection (0.1% relative abundance) in at least 2 samples (n = 14943 ASVs excluded).

# VGO3 NP & OP 
# Remove low abundant ASVs and convert counts to relative abundances
ps_vgo3_npop_filtered <- microbiome_VGO3_NPOP %>% 
  pres_abund_filter() %>% 
  to_RA()
# A total of 1810 ASVs were found to be present at or above a level of confident detection (0.1% relative abundance) in at least 2 samples (n = 14220 ASVs excluded).

```
# Extract OTU and metadata 
```{r}
otu_vgo3_np <- t(as.data.frame(otu_table(ps_vgo3_np_filtered)))
meta_vgo3_np <- meta_to_df(ps_vgo3_np_filtered, sample_name = ".row_id")

otu_vgo3_op <- t(as.data.frame(otu_table(ps_vgo3_op_filtered)))
meta_vgo3_op <- meta_to_df(ps_vgo3_op_filtered, sample_name = ".row_id")

otu_vgo3_npop <- t(as.data.frame(otu_table(ps_vgo3_npop_filtered)))
meta_vgo3_npop <- meta_to_df(ps_vgo3_npop_filtered, sample_name = ".row_id")

```
# Check associations between variables
```{r}
# Select lung function variables
lung_vars <- c("FEV1_pred", "FVC_pred", "FEV1FVC_pred", "MMEF_pred")

sd <- sample_data(microbiome_VGO3_NP)
df_lung <- sd[, lung_vars]

# Calculate correlation matrix with p-values
cor_res <- rcorr(as.matrix(df_lung), type = "pearson")  # Hmisc package
cor_matrix <- round(cor_res$r, 3)
p_matrix <- cor_res$P

# Melt the matrices
melted_cor <- melt(cor_matrix)
melted_p <- melt(p_matrix)

# Merge correlation values and p-values
melted_cor$p_value <- melted_p$value

# Function to assign significance stars
melted_cor$signif <- cut(
  melted_cor$p_value,
  breaks = c(-Inf, 0.001, 0.01, 0.05, Inf),
  labels = c("***", "**", "*", "")
)

# Keep only lower triangle (exclude duplicates & diagonal)
melted_cor <- melted_cor[as.numeric(melted_cor$Var1) > as.numeric(melted_cor$Var2), ]

# Plot enhanced lower triangular heatmap
lung_func_corr <- ggplot(melted_cor, aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = value)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(sprintf("%.2f", value), signif)), color = "black", size = 4) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#2166AC",   # blue for negative correlations
    mid = "white",     # neutral = 0
    high = "#B2182B",  # red for positive correlations
    midpoint = 0,
    limits = c(-1, 1),
    name = "Pearson r"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Correlation Matrix of Lung Function\n Outcomes with Significance",
       x = "", y = "") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )

ggsave("../Output/Variable_associations/Lung_function_correlations.svg", width = 5, height = 5)


# Test associations between variables to be included in multivariable models
vars <- c("Age", "Gender", "season_microbiome_sampling", "Smoking_status", "Asthma_current", "COPD_emphy_doc_diag", "Farm_childhood")
str(sd$Age)
str(sd$Gender)
str(sd$season_microbiome_sampling)
str(sd$Smoking_status)
str(sd$Asthma_current)
str(sd$COPD_emphy_doc_diag)
str(sd$Farm_childhood)


# Check data types
sapply(sd[vars], class)

# Initialize results dataframe
results <- data.frame(
  var1 = character(),
  var2 = character(),
  test = character(),
  p_value = numeric(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Loop through all pairs
for (i in 1:(length(vars)-1)) {
  for (j in (i+1):length(vars)) {
    v1 <- sd[[vars[i]]]
    v2 <- sd[[vars[j]]]
    
    # Continuous vs continuous
    if (is.numeric(v1) & is.numeric(v2)) {
      test_res <- cor.test(v1, v2)
      results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
        var1 = vars[i],
        var2 = vars[j],
        test = "Pearson",
        p_value = test_res$p.value
      ))
    }
    
    # Continuous vs factor
    else if ((is.numeric(v1) & is.factor(v2)) | (is.factor(v1) & is.numeric(v2))) {
      if (is.factor(v1)) { tmp <- v1; v1 <- v2; v2 <- tmp; }
      if (nlevels(v2) == 2) {
        test_res <- t.test(v1 ~ v2)
        test_name <- "t-test"
        pval <- test_res$p.value
      } else {
        test_res <- aov(v1 ~ v2)
        test_name <- "ANOVA"
        pval <- summary(test_res)[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1]
      }
      results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
        var1 = vars[i],
        var2 = vars[j],
        test = test_name,
        p_value = pval
      ))
    }
    
    # Factor vs factor
    else if (is.factor(v1) & is.factor(v2)) {
      test_res <- chisq.test(table(v1, v2))
      results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
        var1 = vars[i],
        var2 = vars[j],
        test = "Chi-squared",
        p_value = test_res$p.value
      ))
    }
  }
}

# Add significance stars
results <- results %>%
  mutate(
    signif = cut(
      p_value,
      breaks = c(-Inf, 0.001, 0.01, 0.05, Inf),
      labels = c("***", "**", "*", "")
    ),
    p_value = signif(round(p_value, 4))
  )

# Arrange results nicely
results <- results %>% arrange(var1, var2)

# View the table
print(results)

# Save the results table to Excel
write_xlsx(results, "../Output/Variable_associations/Covariate_association_results.xlsx")

```


# Alpha diversity
We run linear models to assess the association between lung function values (% predicted lung function measures (FEV1, FVC, FEV1/FVC) and alpha diversity (Observed, Shannon, Simpson)
## Visualise associations between lung function variables and diversity
```{r}
# Input
datasets <- list(
  VGO3_NP = meta_vgo3_np,
  VGO3_OP = meta_vgo3_op
)

# Variables of interest 
lung_vars     <- c("FEV1_pred", "FVC_pred", "FEV1FVC_pred", "MMEF_pred")
alpha_metrics <- c("Observed", "Shannon", "Simpson")

# Pretty axis labels for lung function
lung_label <- c(
  FEV1_pred    = "FEV1 % predicted",
  FVC_pred     = "FVC % predicted",
  FEV1FVC_pred = "FEV1/FVC % predicted",
  MMEF_pred    = "MMEF % predicted"  # aka MMEF
)

# Plot styling
base_theme <- theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 11)
  )
point_col <- "#2c7fb8"

# Helper: single scatter with Spearman annotation 
make_scatterplot <- function(df, xvar, yvar, dataset_name) {
  # Coerce to numeric + keep complete cases
  df_sub <- df %>%
    mutate(
      !!xvar := suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[[xvar]])),
      !!yvar := suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[[yvar]]))
    ) %>%
    filter(is.finite(.data[[xvar]]), is.finite(.data[[yvar]]))

  if (nrow(df_sub) < 3) return(NULL)

  # Spearman correlation
  ct <- suppressWarnings(stats::cor.test(df_sub[[xvar]], df_sub[[yvar]], method = "spearman"))
  rho_lab <- paste0(
    "\u03C1 = ", sprintf("%.2f", as.numeric(ct$estimate)),
    ", p = ", format.pval(ct$p.value, digits = 2)
  )

  # Axis labels (fallback to variable name if not in lung_label)
  x_lab <- if (!is.null(lung_label[[xvar]])) lung_label[[xvar]] else xvar

  ggplot(df_sub, aes(x = .data[[xvar]], y = .data[[yvar]])) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 1.7, color = point_col) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black", linetype = "dashed") +
    labs(
      title = paste(yvar, "vs", x_lab, "\n", dataset_name),
      x = x_lab,
      y = yvar
    ) +
    # Use text (not label) to avoid label.size warning on some ggplot2 versions
    annotate("text", x = -Inf, y = Inf, hjust = -0.05, vjust = 1.2,
             label = rho_lab, size = 3.2) +
    base_theme
}

# Build plots for each dataset
scatter_plots <- list()

# Optional: collector for a Spearman summary table
spearman_rows <- list()

for (dataset_name in names(datasets)) {
  df0 <- datasets[[dataset_name]]

  # Ensure the columns we use are numeric where needed
  for (a in alpha_metrics) {
    if (a %in% names(df0)) df0[[a]] <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(df0[[a]]))
  }
  for (lv in lung_vars) {
    if (lv %in% names(df0)) df0[[lv]] <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(df0[[lv]]))
  }

  plot_list <- list()

  for (lung_var in intersect(lung_vars, names(df0))) {
    for (alpha_var in intersect(alpha_metrics, names(df0))) {
      # Make plot
      p <- make_scatterplot(df0, lung_var, alpha_var, dataset_name)
      if (!is.null(p)) {
        plot_id <- paste(dataset_name, lung_var, alpha_var, sep = " :: ")
        plot_list[[plot_id]] <- p
      }

      # Add to summary table (only if we have ≥3 complete cases)
      df_sub <- df0 %>%
        filter(is.finite(.data[[lung_var]]), is.finite(.data[[alpha_var]]))
      if (nrow(df_sub) >= 3) {
        ct <- suppressWarnings(stats::cor.test(df_sub[[lung_var]], df_sub[[alpha_var]], method = "spearman"))
        spearman_rows[[length(spearman_rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
          dataset = dataset_name,
          lung_var = lung_var,
          alpha_metric = alpha_var,
          n = nrow(df_sub),
          rho = unname(as.numeric(ct$estimate)),
          p_value = unname(ct$p.value),
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        )
      }
    }
  }

  # Wrap plots for this dataset
  if (length(plot_list)) {
    scatter_plots[[dataset_name]] <-
      patchwork::wrap_plots(plot_list, ncol = 3) +
      patchwork::plot_annotation(
        title = paste("Alpha diversity vs lung function:", dataset_name),
        theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"))
      )
  }
}

# Show and save 
outdir <- "../Output/Cross_sectional/Alpha_diversity/Boxplots_scatterplots"  

dir.create(outdir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

for (nm in names(scatter_plots)) {
  fig <- scatter_plots[[nm]]
  print(fig)
  ggsave(
    filename = file.path(outdir, paste0(nm, "_scatterplots.svg")),
    plot     = fig,
    width    = 14,
    height   = 10
  )
}

# export Spearman summary table
if (length(spearman_rows)) {
  spearman_summary <- dplyr::bind_rows(spearman_rows)
  readr::write_csv(spearman_summary, file.path(outdir, "spearman_summary.csv"))
}
```
## Test whether stratification on respiratory disease is recommended
- Fit two models for every combo (NP/OP × alpha × lung):
-- Pooled model: alpha ~ lung + disease + covariates
-- Interaction model: alpha ~ lung × disease + covariates → from this, it also calculates the separate slopes for the no-disease and disease groups.
- It BH-adjusts the p-values and decides: stratify only if the interaction q < 0.10.
```{r}
# Alpha diversity — pooled vs interaction models (NP/OP)
# Decision: stratify if q_interaction < 0.10
alpha_vars       <- c("Observed","Shannon","Simpson")
lung_fun_cont    <- c("FEV1_pred","FVC_pred","FEV1FVC_pred","MMEF_pred")
core_covariates  <- c("Age","Gender","Smoking_status","season_microbiome_sampling")
q_interaction_thresh <- 0.10

# ---- Pretty labels ----
pretty_alpha <- c(
  "Observed_z" = "Observed richness",
  "Shannon_z"  = "Shannon diversity",
  "Simpson_z"  = "Simpson diversity"
)
pretty_lung <- c(
  "FEV1_pred_decline_z"     = "FEV1 % predicted (decline)",
  "FVC_pred_decline_z"      = "FVC % predicted (decline)",
  "FEV1FVC_pred_decline_z"  = "FEV1/FVC % predicted (decline)",
  "MMEF_pred_decline_z"     = "MMEF % predicted (decline)"
)

# ---- Your RespDx builder (used only if RespDx not present) ----
make_respdx <- function(meta) {
  to_yesno <- function(x) dplyr::case_when(
    x %in% c(1,"1","yes","Yes","YES",TRUE) ~ "yes",
    x %in% c(0,"0","no","No","NO",FALSE)   ~ "no",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  )
  a <- to_yesno(meta$Asthma_current)
  c <- to_yesno(meta$COPD_emphy_doc_diag)
  y <- ifelse((!is.na(a) & a=="yes") | (!is.na(c) & c=="yes"), "yes",
              ifelse(!is.na(a) | !is.na(c), "no", NA_character_))
  factor(y, levels = c("no","yes"))
}

# ---- Preprocess: cast types, make alpha *_z, lung *_decline_z, and ensure RespDx factor ----
preprocess_dataset <- function(df) {
  df <- df %>%
    mutate(
      Age           = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(Age))),
      FEV1_pred     = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(FEV1_pred))),
      FVC_pred      = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(FVC_pred))),
      FEV1FVC_pred  = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(FEV1FVC_pred))),
      MMEF_pred     = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(MMEF_pred))),
      Observed      = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(Observed))),
      Shannon       = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(Shannon))),
      Simpson       = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(Simpson))),
      Gender = factor(as.character(Gender), levels = c("Male","Female")),
      Smoking_status = factor(tolower(as.character(Smoking_status)),
                              levels = c("never","former","current"), ordered = TRUE),
      season_microbiome_sampling = factor(as.character(season_microbiome_sampling),
                              levels = c("Winter","Spring","Summer","Autumn"), ordered = TRUE),
      Asthma_current = dplyr::case_when(
        Asthma_current %in% c(1,"1","yes","Yes","YES",TRUE) ~ "yes",
        Asthma_current %in% c(0,"0","no","No","NO",FALSE)   ~ "no",
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      ),
      COPD_emphy_doc_diag = dplyr::case_when(
        COPD_emphy_doc_diag %in% c(1,"1","yes","Yes","YES",TRUE) ~ "yes",
        COPD_emphy_doc_diag %in% c(0,"0","no","No","NO",FALSE)   ~ "no",
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      )
    )

  # alpha z-scores
  have_alpha <- intersect(alpha_vars, names(df))
  if (length(have_alpha)) {
    df <- df %>% mutate(across(all_of(have_alpha), ~ as.numeric(scale(.x)), .names = "{.col}_z"))
  }

  # flip lung (higher = worse) then z-score
  have_lung <- intersect(lung_fun_cont, names(df))
  if (length(have_lung)) {
    df <- df %>% mutate(across(all_of(have_lung), ~ as.numeric(scale(-.x)), .names = "{.col}_decline_z"))
  }

  # Use existing RespDx if present; otherwise build it
  if ("RespDx" %in% names(df)) {
    df$RespDx <- factor(as.character(df$RespDx), levels = c("no","yes"))
  } else {
    df$RespDx <- make_respdx(df)
    df$RespDx <- droplevels(df$RespDx)
  }

  df
}

# ---- Helper: per-stratum slopes from interaction model ----
slope_with_se <- function(fit, lung_col) {
  cf  <- coef(fit); V <- vcov(fit); dfR <- df.residual(fit)

  # slope in RespDx == "no"
  b1  <- unname(cf[lung_col])
  se1 <- sqrt(unname(V[lung_col, lung_col]))
  t1  <- b1 / se1; p1 <- 2*pt(-abs(t1), df=dfR)

  # interaction term can appear in either order
  int_regex <- paste0("^(", lung_col, ":RespDxyes|RespDxyes:", lung_col, ")$")
  int_name  <- grep(int_regex, names(cf), value = TRUE)
  if (!length(int_name)) {
    return(list(b_no=b1, se_no=se1, p_no=p1,
                b_yes=NA_real_, se_yes=NA_real_, p_yes=NA_real_))
  }

  b3 <- unname(cf[int_name])
  var_sum <- unname(V[lung_col, lung_col] + V[int_name, int_name] + 2*V[lung_col, int_name])
  se2 <- sqrt(var_sum)
  b2  <- b1 + b3  # slope in RespDx == "yes"
  t2  <- b2 / se2; p2 <- 2*pt(-abs(t2), df=dfR)

  list(b_no=b1, se_no=se1, p_no=p1,
       b_yes=b2, se_yes=se2, p_yes=p2)
}

# ---- Fit pooled & interaction models for one dataset ----
fit_overall_and_interaction <- function(df, dataset_name) {
  df <- preprocess_dataset(df)

  alpha_z_vars     <- c("Observed_z","Shannon_z","Simpson_z")
  lung_decline_var <- paste0(lung_fun_cont, "_decline_z")

  out <- list()

  for (alpha_col in alpha_z_vars) {
    if (!alpha_col %in% names(df)) next
    for (lung_col in lung_decline_var) {
      if (!lung_col %in% names(df)) next

      needed <- c(alpha_col, lung_col, "RespDx", core_covariates)
      if (!all(needed %in% names(df))) next

      dsub <- df %>% dplyr::select(all_of(needed)) %>% tidyr::drop_na()
      if (!nrow(dsub)) next
      dsub$RespDx <- droplevels(dsub$RespDx)
      if (nlevels(dsub$RespDx) < 2) next  # need both strata

      # Pooled model: alpha ~ lung + RespDx + covariates
      form0 <- as.formula(paste(alpha_col, "~", paste(c(lung_col, "RespDx", core_covariates), collapse = " + ")))
      m0 <- tryCatch(lm(form0, data = dsub), error = function(e) NULL)
      if (is.null(m0)) next

      # Interaction model: alpha ~ lung*RespDx + covariates
      form1 <- as.formula(paste(alpha_col, "~", paste(c(paste0(lung_col, "*RespDx"), core_covariates), collapse = " + ")))
      m1 <- tryCatch(lm(form1, data = dsub), error = function(e) NULL)
      if (is.null(m1)) next

      # Extract pooled lung effect
      td0  <- broom::tidy(m0)
      row0 <- td0 %>% dplyr::filter(term == lung_col) %>% dplyr::slice_tail(n = 1)

      # Extract interaction p-value (order-robust)
      td1 <- broom::tidy(m1)
      int_regex <- paste0("^(", lung_col, ":RespDxyes|RespDxyes:", lung_col, ")$")
      row_int <- td1 %>% dplyr::filter(grepl(int_regex, term)) %>% dplyr::slice_tail(n = 1)

      # Per-stratum slopes from m1
      sws <- slope_with_se(m1, lung_col)

      # Ns
      n_total <- stats::nobs(m1)
      tab_str <- table(dsub$RespDx)
      n_no  <- as.integer(tab_str["no"]);  if (is.na(n_no))  n_no  <- 0L
      n_yes <- as.integer(tab_str["yes"]); if (is.na(n_yes)) n_yes <- 0L

      out[[paste(alpha_col, lung_col, sep="__")]] <- tibble(
        dataset      = dataset_name,
        alpha_metric = alpha_col,
        lung_var     = lung_col,
        n_total = n_total, n_no = n_no, n_yes = n_yes,

        beta_overall = row0$estimate, se_overall = row0$std.error, p_overall = row0$p.value,
        p_interaction = if (nrow(row_int)) row_int$p.value else NA_real_,

        beta_no  = sws$b_no,  se_no  = sws$se_no,  p_no  = sws$p_no,
        beta_yes = sws$b_yes, se_yes = sws$se_yes, p_yes = sws$p_yes
      )
    }
  }

  dplyr::bind_rows(out)
}

# ---- Run across datasets ----
stopifnot(exists("dataset_list"), length(dataset_list) >= 1)
all_res <- lapply(names(dataset_list), function(nm) fit_overall_and_interaction(dataset_list[[nm]], nm)) %>%
  bind_rows()

if (!nrow(all_res)) stop("No models could be fit. Check that alpha *_z, lung *_decline_z, and RespDx exist and that both strata are present after NA filtering.")

# ---- BH adjust per dataset; labels; decision ----
label_stars <- function(p) dplyr::case_when(
  is.na(p) ~ "",
  p < 0.05 ~ "***",
  p < 0.10 ~ "**",
  p < 0.25 ~ "*",
  TRUE     ~ ""
)

res_labeled <- all_res %>%
  mutate(
    alpha_metric = dplyr::recode(alpha_metric, !!!pretty_alpha),
    lung_var     = dplyr::recode(lung_var,     !!!pretty_lung),
    niche        = dplyr::recode(dataset, VGO3_NP = "NP", VGO3_OP = "OP")
  ) %>%
  group_by(dataset) %>%
  mutate(
    p_overall_adj     = p.adjust(p_overall,     method = "BH"),
    p_interaction_adj = p.adjust(p_interaction, method = "BH"),
    p_no_adj          = p.adjust(p_no,          method = "BH"),
    p_yes_adj         = p.adjust(p_yes,         method = "BH"),

    star_overall     = label_stars(p_overall_adj),
    star_interaction = label_stars(p_interaction_adj),
    star_no          = label_stars(p_no_adj),
    star_yes         = label_stars(p_yes_adj),

    stratify_0_10 = ifelse(!is.na(p_interaction_adj) & p_interaction_adj < q_interaction_thresh,
                           "Stratify", "No stratify")
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# ---- Save tidy table ----
out_dir <- "../Output/Cross_sectional/Alpha_diversity/Strata_decision"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

readr::write_csv(
  res_labeled %>%
    select(dataset, niche, alpha_metric, lung_var,
           n_total, n_no, n_yes,
           beta_overall, se_overall, p_overall, p_overall_adj, star_overall,
           p_interaction, p_interaction_adj, star_interaction,
           beta_no, se_no, p_no, p_no_adj, star_no,
           beta_yes, se_yes, p_yes, p_yes_adj, star_yes,
           stratify_0_10),
  file.path(out_dir, "Multivariable_alpha_diversity_overall_interaction.csv")
)

# ---- Heatmap of interaction q-values ----
int_df <- res_labeled %>%
  mutate(
    lung_var = factor(lung_var, levels = c(
      "FEV1 % predicted (decline)",
      "FVC % predicted (decline)",
      "FEV1/FVC % predicted (decline)",
      "MMEF % predicted (decline)"
    )),
    alpha_metric = factor(alpha_metric, levels = c(
      "Observed richness","Shannon diversity","Simpson diversity"
    )),
    neglog10_padj_int = -log10(pmin(p_interaction_adj, 1))
  )

heat_int <- ggplot(int_df, aes(x = alpha_metric, y = lung_var, fill = neglog10_padj_int)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = star_interaction), size = 4) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "grey95", high = "grey20", name = expression(-log[10]("(adj p)"))) +
  facet_wrap(~ niche, nrow = 1) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold")) +
  labs(title = "Interaction test (RespDx × lung function) — should you stratify?",
       x = "Alpha diversity metric", y = "Lung function metric (decline)")

ggsave(file.path(out_dir, "alpha_diversity_interaction_heatmap.svg"),
       heat_int, width = 7.5, height = 3.8)

# ---- Quick console summary ----
cat("\nStratify decisions (q_interaction < 0.10):\n")
print(res_labeled %>% count(dataset, niche, stratify_0_10))

```
## Non-stratified analyses 
### Univariable
```{r}
# Inputs
dataset_list <- list(VGO3_NP = meta_vgo3_np, VGO3_OP = meta_vgo3_op)
alpha_vars   <- c("Observed","Shannon","Simpson")

# Predictors to test (includes RespDx)
test_vars <- c(
  "Age","Gender","BMI","season_microbiome_sampling","Education_level",
  "Smoking_status","RespDx","Covid_lastmonth","Atopic","Cortinhal_lastyr","Pneumonia_lastyr",
  "Antibiotics_lastmonth","Antibiotics_lastyr","Farm_childhood","T1_wkprior_NH3",
  "FEV1_pred","FVC_pred","FEV1FVC_pred","MMEF_pred"
)

# Pretty labels
pretty_labels <- c(
  "Age"="Age","Gender"="Gender (Female vs Male)","BMI"="Body Mass Index",
  "season_microbiome_sampling"="Season of sampling","Education_level"="Education level",
  "Smoking_status"="Smoking status","RespDx"="Respiratory disease (COPD/asthma)",
  "Covid_lastmonth"="COVID-19 in last month","Atopic"="Atopic",
  "Cortinhal_lastyr"="Corticosteroid inhaler (last year)",
  "Pneumonia_lastyr"="Pneumonia in last year",
  "Antibiotics_lastmonth"="Antibiotics last month","Antibiotics_lastyr"="Antibiotics last year",
  "Farm_childhood"="Grew up on a farm","T1_wkprior_NH3"="NH3 week prior to sampling",
  "FEV1_pred"="FEV1 % predicted (decline)","FVC_pred"="FVC % predicted (decline)",
  "FEV1FVC_pred"="FEV1/FVC % predicted (decline)","MMEF_pred"="MMEF % predicted (decline)",
  "Observed"="Observed richness","Shannon"="Shannon diversity","Simpson"="Simpson diversity"
)

desired_order <- c(
  "Age","Gender (Female vs Male)","Body Mass Index","Season of sampling","Education level",
  "Smoking status","Respiratory disease (COPD/asthma)","Pneumonia in last year","COVID-19 in last month","Atopic",
  "Corticosteroid inhaler (last year)","Antibiotics last month","Antibiotics last year",
  "Grew up on a farm","NH3 week prior to sampling",
  "FEV1 % predicted (decline)","FVC % predicted (decline)",
  "FEV1/FVC % predicted (decline)","MMEF % predicted (decline)"
)

lung_fun_cont <- c("FEV1_pred","FVC_pred","FEV1FVC_pred","MMEF_pred")

# ---- Preprocess: coerce types; ensure existing RespDx; z-score alpha ----
preprocess_dataset <- function(df){
  df <- df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      Age = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(Age))),
      BMI = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(BMI))),
      T1_wkprior_NH3 = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(T1_wkprior_NH3))),
      FEV1_pred = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(FEV1_pred))),
      FVC_pred  = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(FVC_pred))),
      FEV1FVC_pred = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(FEV1FVC_pred))),
      MMEF_pred = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(MMEF_pred))),
      Observed  = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(Observed))),
      Shannon   = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(Shannon))),
      Simpson   = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(Simpson))),
      Gender = factor(as.character(Gender), levels = c("Male","Female")),
      Smoking_status = factor(tolower(as.character(Smoking_status)),
                              levels = c("never","former","current"), ordered = TRUE),
      Education_level = factor(tolower(as.character(Education_level)),
                               levels = c("low","medium","high"), ordered = TRUE),
      season_microbiome_sampling = factor(as.character(season_microbiome_sampling),
                               levels = c("Winter","Spring","Summer","Autumn"), ordered = TRUE)
    )

  # Use existing RespDx only
  if (!"RespDx" %in% names(df)) stop("RespDx not found in data; please add it first.")
  df$RespDx <- factor(tolower(as.character(df$RespDx)), levels = c("no","yes"))

  df <- df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      Observed_z = as.numeric(scale(Observed)),
      Shannon_z  = as.numeric(scale(Shannon)),
      Simpson_z  = as.numeric(scale(Simpson))
    )
  df
}

# Standardize any predictor to numeric
x_to_std <- function(x){
  if (is.factor(x) && is.ordered(x))            return(as.numeric(scale(as.numeric(x) - 1L)))
  if (is.factor(x) && nlevels(droplevels(x))==2) return(as.numeric(scale(as.numeric(x != levels(x)[1]))))
  if (is.numeric(x))                             return(as.numeric(scale(x)))
  return(NULL)
}

# ---- Core: unadjusted univariable models ----
run_univ_unadjusted <- function(df, dataset_name){
  df <- preprocess_dataset(df)
  alpha_z <- c("Observed_z","Shannon_z","Simpson_z")

  purrr::map_dfr(alpha_z, function(a){
    y <- df[[a]]
    purrr::map_dfr(intersect(test_vars, names(df)), function(v){
      xs <- x_to_std(df[[v]])
      if (is.null(xs)) return(tibble::tibble())
      dat <- tibble::tibble(y = as.numeric(y), xs = as.numeric(xs)) %>% tidyr::drop_na()
      if (nrow(dat) < 10) return(tibble::tibble())

      fit <- tryCatch(stats::lm(y ~ xs, data = dat), error = function(e) NULL)
      if (is.null(fit)) return(tibble::tibble())

      row <- broom::tidy(fit) %>% dplyr::filter(term == "xs")
      if (!nrow(row)) return(tibble::tibble())

      tibble::tibble(
        dataset = dataset_name,
        niche   = dplyr::recode(dataset_name, VGO3_NP="NP", VGO3_OP="OP"),
        alpha_metric = a,
        variable = v,
        estimate = row$estimate, std.error = row$std.error, p.value = row$p.value,
        n_used = stats::nobs(fit)
      )
    })
  })
}

univ_all <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  run_univ_unadjusted(dataset_list$VGO3_NP, "VGO3_NP"),
  run_univ_unadjusted(dataset_list$VGO3_OP, "VGO3_OP")
)

# Optional: flip sign for lung (higher = worse)
univ_all <- univ_all %>% dplyr::mutate(estimate = dplyr::if_else(variable %in% lung_fun_cont, -estimate, estimate))

# Labels + BH (within dataset × alpha metric)
univ_all <- univ_all %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    alpha_metric = dplyr::recode(alpha_metric,
      Observed_z="Observed richness", Shannon_z="Shannon diversity", Simpson_z="Simpson diversity"),
    variable_lab = dplyr::recode(variable, !!!pretty_labels, .default = variable)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(dataset, alpha_metric) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(q.value = p.adjust(p.value, method="BH"),
                stars = dplyr::case_when(q.value<=0.05~"***", q.value<=0.10~"**", q.value<=0.25~"*", TRUE~"")) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

# Plot heatmap
pal <- try(rcartocolor::carto_pal(7, "Geyser"), silent=TRUE)
if (inherits(pal, "try-error")) pal <- c("#2166ac","#67a9cf","#d1e5f0","#f7f7f7","#fddbc7","#ef8a62","#b2182b")
lim <- max(abs(univ_all$estimate), na.rm=TRUE); if (!is.finite(lim)) lim <- 1

plot_df <- univ_all %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    variable_lab = factor(variable_lab, levels = rev(intersect(desired_order, unique(variable_lab)))),
    niche = factor(niche, levels=c("NP","OP")),
    alpha_metric = factor(alpha_metric, levels=c("Observed richness","Shannon diversity","Simpson diversity"))
  )

p_univ <- ggplot2::ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = alpha_metric, y = variable_lab, fill = estimate)) +
  ggplot2::geom_tile(color="white") +
  ggplot2::geom_text(aes(label = stars), size=3.8) +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_gradient2(low=pal[1], mid=pal[4], high=pal[7], midpoint=0, limits=c(-lim, lim),
                                name="Std. β") +
  ggplot2::facet_wrap(~ niche, nrow=1, scales="free_y") +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size=12) +
  ggplot2::theme(panel.grid=element_blank(), axis.text.x=element_text(angle=30,hjust=1),
                 strip.text=element_text(face="bold")) +
  ggplot2::labs(x="Alpha diversity metric", y="Predictor")

dir.create("../Output/Cross_sectional/Alpha_diversity/Univariable/Non_stratified", recursive=TRUE, showWarnings=FALSE)
ggplot2::ggsave("../Output/Cross_sectional/Alpha_diversity/Univariable/Non_stratified/Univariable_alpha_heatmap_nonstratified.svg",
                p_univ, width=7.5, height=5)

readr::write_csv(univ_all,
  "../Output/Cross_sectional/Alpha_diversity/Univariable/Non_stratified/Univariable_alpha_results_nonstratified.csv")

```
### Multivariable
```{r}
dataset_list <- list(VGO3_NP = meta_vgo3_np, VGO3_OP = meta_vgo3_op)
alpha_vars   <- c("Observed","Shannon","Simpson")
lung_fun_cont <- c("FEV1_pred","FVC_pred","FEV1FVC_pred","MMEF_pred")
core_covariates <- c("Age","Gender","Smoking_status","season_microbiome_sampling")

pretty_alpha <- c("Observed_z"="Observed richness","Shannon_z"="Shannon diversity","Simpson_z"="Simpson diversity")
pretty_lung  <- c("FEV1_pred_decline_z"="FEV1 % predicted (decline)",
                  "FVC_pred_decline_z"="FVC % predicted (decline)",
```
