# Composition plots
## Top 5 ASVs per niche 
```{r}
# Merge NP + OP
ps_combined <- merge_phyloseq(ps_vgo3_np_filtered, ps_vgo3_op_filtered)

#Transform to relative abundance 
ps_rel <- transform_sample_counts(ps_combined, function(x) x / sum(x))

# Extract data
otu_df <- as.data.frame(otu_table(ps_rel))
meta_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps_rel))

# Ensure sample order alignment
if (!identical(colnames(otu_df), rownames(meta_df))) {
  otu_df <- otu_df[, rownames(meta_df)]
}

# Average per niche
meta_df$niche <- as.factor(meta_df$niche)
otu_avg <- sapply(levels(meta_df$niche), function(niche) {
  rowMeans(otu_df[, rownames(meta_df[meta_df$niche == niche, ]), drop = FALSE])
})
otu_avg <- as.data.frame(otu_avg)
otu_avg$Taxon <- rownames(otu_avg)

#  Compute top 5 per niche
top_np <- otu_avg %>% arrange(desc(NP)) %>% slice_head(n = 5) %>% pull(Taxon)
top_op <- otu_avg %>% arrange(desc(OP)) %>% slice_head(n = 5) %>% pull(Taxon)

# Reshape for plotting
otu_long <- otu_avg %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = c("NP", "OP"), names_to = "Niche", values_to = "Abundance") %>%
  mutate(TaxonGroup = case_when(
    Niche == "NP" & Taxon %in% top_np ~ Taxon,
    Niche == "OP" & Taxon %in% top_op ~ Taxon,
    TRUE ~ "Other"
  ))

# Average proportions within each Niche
df_plot <- otu_long %>%
  group_by(Niche, TaxonGroup) %>%
  summarise(MeanRelAbundance = sum(Abundance), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(Niche) %>%
  mutate(Proportion = MeanRelAbundance / sum(MeanRelAbundance))


# Properly order taxa within each niche and move "Other" to top ----
df_plot <- df_plot %>%
  group_by(Niche) %>%
  arrange(Niche, Proportion) %>%
  mutate(TaxonGroup = forcats::fct_reorder2(
    TaxonGroup, Niche, Proportion, .desc = FALSE
  ))

# Create a unified ordering for plotting and legend:
# Within each niche: from least → most abundant (so bars stack bottom→top)
# Move "Other" to be plotted last (top of bar) and last in legend
unique_taxa_order <- df_plot %>%
  arrange(Niche, Proportion) %>%
  distinct(TaxonGroup) %>%
  pull(TaxonGroup)
unique_taxa_order <- c(setdiff(unique_taxa_order, "Other"), "Other")

df_plot$TaxonGroup <- factor(df_plot$TaxonGroup, levels = unique_taxa_order)

# Plot
palette_colors <- brewer.pal(10, "Paired")
unique_taxa <- unique(df_plot$TaxonGroup)
palette_final <- setNames(rep(palette_colors, length.out = length(unique_taxa)), unique_taxa)
palette_final["Other"] <- "grey80"

ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = Niche, y = Proportion, fill = TaxonGroup)) +
  geom_col(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = palette_final, breaks = unique_taxa_order) +  # ensures legend order
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(
    title = "Top 5 ASVs per niche (each niche independent)",
    y = "Relative abundance",
    x = NULL,
    fill = "ASV"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9)
  )

# Save
ggsave(
  "../Output/Cross_sectional/Microbiome_descriptives/Fig_VGO3_NP_OP_top5_ASV_per_niche_final.svg",
  width = 5, height = 4, dpi = 300
)

```

## Top 5 genera per niche 
```{r}
# Merge NP + OP genus-level datasets
ps_combined_genus <- merge_phyloseq(ps_vgo3_np_filtered, ps_vgo3_op_filtered)

# Transform to relative abundance 
ps_rel_genus <- transform_sample_counts(ps_combined_genus, function(x) x / sum(x))

# Collapse taxa to Genus level (this step ensures the plot is at genus level)
ps_genus <- tax_glom(ps_rel_genus, taxrank = "Genus", NArm = TRUE)

# Extract abundance and metadata
otu_df <- as.data.frame(otu_table(ps_genus))
meta_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps_genus))

# Ensure sample order alignment
if (!identical(colnames(otu_df), rownames(meta_df))) {
  otu_df <- otu_df[, rownames(meta_df)]
}

# Average per niche
meta_df$niche <- as.factor(meta_df$niche)
otu_avg <- sapply(levels(meta_df$niche), function(niche) {
  rowMeans(otu_df[, rownames(meta_df[meta_df$niche == niche, ]), drop = FALSE])
})
otu_avg <- as.data.frame(otu_avg)

# Add genus names
tax_df <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ps_genus))
otu_avg$Genus <- tax_df$Genus
otu_avg$Genus[is.na(otu_avg$Genus)] <- "Unclassified"
otu_avg$Genus <- make.unique(otu_avg$Genus)  # Ensure unique genus names

# Compute top 5 per niche
top_np <- otu_avg %>% arrange(desc(NP)) %>% slice_head(n = 5) %>% pull(Genus)
top_op <- otu_avg %>% arrange(desc(OP)) %>% slice_head(n = 5) %>% pull(Genus)

# Reshape for plotting
otu_long <- otu_avg %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = c("NP", "OP"), names_to = "Niche", values_to = "Abundance") %>%
  mutate(TaxonGroup = case_when(
    Niche == "NP" & Genus %in% top_np ~ Genus,
    Niche == "OP" & Genus %in% top_op ~ Genus,
    TRUE ~ "Other"
  ))

# Average proportions within each Niche
df_plot <- otu_long %>%
  group_by(Niche, TaxonGroup) %>%
  summarise(MeanRelAbundance = sum(Abundance), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(Niche) %>%
  mutate(Proportion = MeanRelAbundance / sum(MeanRelAbundance))

# Properly order taxa within each niche and move "Other" to top
df_plot <- df_plot %>%
  group_by(Niche) %>%
  arrange(Niche, Proportion) %>%
  mutate(TaxonGroup = forcats::fct_reorder2(
    TaxonGroup, Niche, Proportion, .desc = FALSE
  ))

# Create unified ordering for plotting and legend (Other last)
unique_taxa_order <- df_plot %>%
  arrange(Niche, Proportion) %>%
  distinct(TaxonGroup) %>%
  pull(TaxonGroup)
unique_taxa_order <- c(setdiff(unique_taxa_order, "Other"), "Other")
df_plot$TaxonGroup <- factor(df_plot$TaxonGroup, levels = unique_taxa_order)

# Plot
palette_colors <- brewer.pal(9, "Paired")
unique_taxa <- unique(df_plot$TaxonGroup)
palette_final <- setNames(rep(palette_colors, length.out = length(unique_taxa)), unique_taxa)
palette_final["Other"] <- "grey80"

ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = Niche, y = Proportion, fill = TaxonGroup)) +
  geom_col(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = palette_final, breaks = unique_taxa_order) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(
    title = "Top 5 genera per niche (each niche independent)",
    y = "Relative abundance",
    x = NULL,
    fill = "Genus"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9)
  )

# Save
ggsave(
  "../Output/Cross_sectional/Microbiome_descriptives/Fig_VGO3_NP_OP_top5_Genus_per_niche_final.svg",
  width = 5, height = 4, dpi = 300
)

```

# Table 1 characteristics

```{r}
ps <- readRDS("../Input/Phyloseq/VGO3_NPOP/1_ps_raw.Rds")
length(unique(ps@sam_data$VGO3_ID)) # 1299


sum(VGO3_metadata$asthma_ever == "yes" & VGO3_metadata$copd_doc_diag == "yes") # 62 with asthma (ever)

summarise_numeric <- function(x) {
  x <- x[!is.na(x)]
  if (!length(x)) return(NA_character_)

  m  <- mean(x)
  s  <- sd(x)
  q  <- quantile(x, probs = c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75))

  sprintf("mean %.2f (SD %.2f); median %.2f [IQR %.2f–%.2f]",
          m, s, q[2], q[1], q[3])
}

summarise_categorical <- function(x) {
  x <- droplevels(as.factor(x))
  if (all(is.na(x))) return(NA_character_)

  n_tot <- sum(!is.na(x))
  lvl_stats <- levels(x) %>%
    map_chr(function(lv) {
      n <- sum(x == lv, na.rm = TRUE)
      pct <- 100 * n / n_tot
      sprintf("%s: %d (%.1f%%)", lv, n, pct)
    })

  paste(lvl_stats, collapse = " | ")
}

# identify var types

is_num <- sapply(VGO3_metadata, is.numeric)

numeric_vars <- names(VGO3_metadata)[is_num]
categorical_vars <- names(VGO3_metadata)[!is_num]

# keep only the variables of interest
vars_of_interest <- c(
  "Age","Ethnicity","Gender","Height","Weight","BMI","Smoking_status",
  "Pneumonia_lastyr","COPD_emphy_doc_diag","Asthma_ever","Covid_lastmonth",
  "Asthma_attk_lastyr","Lung_med","Asthma_med_current","Cortinhal_lastyr",
  "Antibiotics_lastmonth","Antibiotics_lastyr","Farm_visit_lastyr",
  "FEV1","FVC","FEV1_FVC","FEF25_75",
  "FEV1_pred","FVC_pred","FEV1FVC_pred","FEF25_75_pred"
)

numeric_vars <- intersect(numeric_vars, vars_of_interest)
categorical_vars <- intersect(categorical_vars, vars_of_interest)

# build summary rows
num_df <- tibble::tibble(
  Variable = numeric_vars,
  Summary  = sapply(VGO3_metadata[numeric_vars], summarise_numeric)
)

cat_df <- tibble::tibble(
  Variable = categorical_vars,
  Summary  = sapply(VGO3_metadata[categorical_vars], summarise_categorical)
)

# bind them and put in a nice order
simple_table <- bind_rows(num_df, cat_df) %>%
  mutate(
    Variable = factor(Variable, levels = vars_of_interest)
  ) %>%
  arrange(Variable) %>%
  mutate(Variable = as.character(Variable))

simple_table


write.csv(simple_table,
          "../Output/Participant_data/Table_VGO3_simple_summary.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)


```
